question_title: Will China’s official NBS non-manufacturing business activity index for April 2026 be greater than 50.0?
summary: China's NBS non-manufacturing PMI for April 2026 needs to be strictly above 50.0 to resolve YES. March 2026 came in at 50.1, providing only a 0.1 buffer above the threshold. Historical base rates strongly favor above-50 readings (85% of months, all 3 previous Aprils above 50), and the conditional analysis when PMI is near 50 shows 79% chance of staying above 50 the next month. However, the seasonal Mar->Apr pattern shows declines in all 3 observed years (-0.4 to -1.8), and the extremely thin margin means even a small decline resolves NO. Recent months have shown unusual weakness with 3 of the last 5 months at or below 50. Trade war headwinds persist but government stimulus provides support. The probability is genuinely uncertain, with the thin margin being the key risk factor. I estimate ~55% YES, giving slightly more weight to the strong historical base rate and recovery momentum than to the limited seasonal pattern.

factors.description: Historical base rate: China's non-manufacturing PMI has been above 50.0 in ~85% of months in our 2023-2026 sample, and in all three previous Aprils (56.4, 51.2, 50.4)
Conditional near-50 analysis: When PMI is between 49.5-51.0, the next month is above 50.0 in 79% of cases (19/24 observations)
Strong seasonal Mar->Apr decline: All 3 years show April declining from March (-1.8, -1.8, -0.4). Even the smallest decline (-0.4) from 50.1 gives 49.7, which resolves NO
Extremely thin buffer: March 2026 at 50.1 means only 0.1 above threshold. Any decline or rounding puts it at or below 50.0
Recent weakness trend: 3 of last 5 months at or below 50.0 (Nov 2025, Jan-Feb 2026), suggesting structural headwinds in services/construction
March recovery momentum: Both manufacturing (50.4) and non-manufacturing (50.1) returned to expansion in March, with improving services subindex (50.2)
Ongoing trade war headwinds: 145% US tariffs on Chinese goods, weak property sector (construction subindex at 49.3 in March), sluggish consumer confidence
Government stimulus support: Stronger government spending in early 2026, policy measures supporting services, resilient AI-related demand

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.55 (55% probability of YES)**

Key reasoning:
- **March 2026 non-manufacturing PMI = 50.1**, barely above the 50.0 threshold
- **Strong historical base rate**: ~85% of months above 50, and all 3 previous Aprils above 50
- **Conditional analysis**: When near 50, 79% chance next month stays above 50 (19/24 observations)
- **Main risk**: Seasonal Mar→Apr decline observed in all 3 years (-0.4 to -1.8), and the razor-thin 0.1 buffer means even a small decline resolves NO
- **Recent weakness**: 3 of last 5 months at or below 50, unusual for this series
- **Mixed macro**: Trade war headwinds offset by government stimulus and recovery momentum

The probability reflects genuine uncertainty — the historical tendency favors YES, but the thin margin and seasonal pattern create substantial downside risk.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will China’s official NBS non-manufacturing business activity index for April 2026 be greater than 50.0?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42928)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the National Bureau of Statistics of China reports a April 2026 non-manufacturing business activity index strictly above 50.0. It resolves to no if the reported value is 50.0 or lower. The primary source is the NBS English press-release page where the monthly PMI release appears: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/
fine_print: Use the first official April 2026 NBS release that includes the non-manufacturing business activity index. If the English page is delayed but the Chinese NBS release is available first, resolve using the corresponding official NBS Chinese release; if a dispute remains, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: NBS release calendar https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/ReleaseCalendar/202512/t20251226_1962154.html ; NBS data portal https://data.stats.gov.cn/english/easyquery.htm?cn=A01
